speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Yea, bloody cloth, I’ll keep thee, for I wished

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Thou shouldst be colored thus. You married ones,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: If each of you should take this course, how many

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Must murder wives much better than themselves

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: For wrying but a little! O Pisanio,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Every good servant does not all commands;

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: No bond but to do just ones. Gods, if you

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Should have ta’en vengeance on my faults, I never

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Had lived to put on this; so had you saved

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: The noble Imogen to repent, and struck

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Me, wretch more worth your vengeance. But, alack,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: You snatch some hence for little faults; that’s love,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: To have them fall no more; you some permit

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: To second ills with ills, each elder worse,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: And make them dread it, to the doers’ thrift.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: But Imogen is your own. Do your best wills,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: And make me blest to obey. I am brought hither

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Among th’ Italian gentry, and to fight

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Against my lady’s kingdom. ’Tis enough

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: That, Britain, I have killed thy mistress. Peace,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: I’ll give no wound to thee. Therefore, good heavens,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Hear patiently my purpose. I’ll disrobe me

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Of these Italian weeds and suit myself

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: As does a Briton peasant. So I’ll fight

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Against the part I come with; so I’ll die

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: For thee, O Imogen, even for whom my life

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Is every breath a death. And thus, unknown,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Pitied nor hated, to the face of peril

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Myself I’ll dedicate. Let me make men know

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: More valor in me than my habits show.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Gods, put the strength o’ th’ Leonati in me.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: To shame the guise o’ th’ world, I will begin

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: The fashion: less without and more within.
